context: Node project with package.json that has scripts: 'typecheck' (passes), 'lint' (FAILS with one error), 'test' (passes). No Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, or go.mod. The script should auto-detect the stack as node-only.
expected_behavior: Exit code 1 (at least one check failed)
Auto-detects 'node' stack from package.json without --stack flag
Runs typecheck, lint, test in order — does NOT short-circuit on first failure (runs all checks so the user sees the full picture)
Each check prints '[PASS] <label>' or '[FAIL] <label>' to stdout
On FAIL, prints the first ~20 lines of the failing command's output for triage
--skip lint excludes lint from the run; exit code is 0 if all OTHER checks pass
--stack node forces node-only detection (no false positives for any present Cargo.toml in a mixed repo)
No recognized stack (no package.json / Cargo.toml / pyproject.toml / go.mod) exits 2 with usage-error stderr message
Output goes to stdout (not stderr) so the caller can pipe / capture; errors-about-the-script-itself go to stderr
Script is executable (chmod +x), not requiring 'bash' prefix
failure_modes: Short-circuits on first FAIL, hiding subsequent failures from the user
Auto-detects stack as 'all' instead of 'node' and runs Rust/Python checks that aren't applicable
Exit code 0 when lint fails — masks the failure
Truncates failure output to 0 lines (just '[FAIL]' with no context for triage)
--skip flag silently ignored, runs all checks anyway
Hard-coded to assume vitest — fails for projects using jest or other test runners
Crashes on package.json with no scripts section
